kept.text:
$NBIS' new inference team is building AEI, or Artificial Efficient Intelligence.

Or just the fastest inference in the world, if you will.

As a long shareholder, $NBIS has impressed me several times, most recently with partnerships with NVIDIA and CrowdStrike.

But I must admit, this has left me completely speechless.

Eigen AI is in a league of its own, and I honestly didn’t think it was buyable.

This is a Kevin Durant to the Golden State Warriors moment for the business.

profile_bio.description: PhD in Engineering Science. Sharing deep insights into great businesses and AI. Educational content, not investment advice.

kept.text:
There are some businesses that are just easier to understand.

Take $CRWD. They get paid upfront, revenue is recurring, and it’s relatively easy to forecast. You can look at revenue, subtract costs like opex and capex, and get a pretty clean sense of what the business earns. It’s not perfect, but directionally it’s simple and gets more predictable over time.

Now compare that to insurance or lending. On day one it looks the same, cash comes in upfront, revenue grows, margins improve, EBITDA looks great, everything feels like it’s working wonderfully. But the economics are completely different.

With SaaS, what you see is mostly what you get. If a customer pays you $100, you deliver the service, and maybe it costs you $30 to run. The outcome is largely known, you make $70. It’s like a gym membership. You collect the fee, you know your costs, and nothing dramatic is hiding in the future.

With lending, that same $100 of “revenue” comes from issuing a loan that won’t fully play out for years. Today it shows up as income. But embedded in that loan is the possibility that $10, $20, or $50 never comes back. You just don’t see it yet. It’s like handing out cash to 100 people and booking the interest as profit on day one, while quietly hoping they all pay you back over time.

That’s the difference. In SaaS, costs are visible. In lending, costs are delayed. Another way to think about it, SaaS is like selling a product and getting paid. Lending is like getting paid today for a promise that might break later.

So in a strong environment, everything looks incredible. You grow loans 30%, revenue jumps, margins expand, EBITDA grows, and it looks like the business is getting better. It feels like skill, like the model is working perfectly.

But what’s actually happening is you may just be taking more risk. Especially if standards a bit, extend to weaker borrowers, push volume, and the numbers will look even better. All these things are difficult to track or monitor and it’s like a casino showing you only the winning hands for a while.

The most difficult part is that none of this shows up immediately. Losses don’t come in smoothly, they come in waves. Everything looks fine until suddenly it doesn’t. That’s why these businesses can look their best right before they prove they weren’t.

You don’t really know what you own when things are going right. You find out when they stop going right. And by then, it’s already in the numbers and it’s too late.

 🌹

profile_bio.description: Equity investor sharing wisdom 🌹

kept.text:
Every $NBIS deal, partnership, acquisition, or fundraise should be viewed through this lens, as Roman explained:

Nothing is ever random. Every step is surgically calculated and serves one goal only: scaling the cloud business.

- The NVIDIA partnership: co-engineering the cloud and inference platform.

- The Meta deal: enabling Nebius to scale its Cloud business.

- The CrowdStrike partnership: bringing cybersecurity to AI cloud infrastructure.

- And now add Eigen AI and lightning-fast inference to this picture, and the strategy becomes crystal clear:

Nebius is building a full-stack AI cloud with a strong software layer, enabling developers at both startups and enterprises to build AI products at scale, with wonderful value-per-dollar economics.

They are doing what they say, and saying what they do.

profile_bio.description: PhD in Engineering Science. Sharing deep insights into great businesses and AI. Educational content, not investment advice.

quoted_tweet.text:
Everybody sees the major news 
- NVIDIA partnership
- META deal
- New fundraise

But not everyone sees how this translates into a product, or why we are doing it.
We are building a full-stack AI cloud, with a strong software layer that enables developers at both startups and enterprises to build AI products at scale.

By the way, 
- if you read the NVIDIA partnership announcement carefully, it is really about co-engineering the cloud and the inference platform.
- if you read Meta deal announcement carefully, it enables us to scale our Cloud business

None of this news is random. We are doing what we say, and saying what we do.

kept.text:
THY–CrowdStrike ilişkisi ve İsrail bağlantısı.

2024 Temmuz’unda dünya çapında binlerce sistem çöktü.
THY de dahil birçok havayolu buradan ciddi manada etkilendi.

Sebep neydi biliyor musunuz?
Bir siber güvenlik şirketi olan: CrowdStrike.

CrowdStrike, ABD merkezli bir firmadır.
Ancak mühendislik altyapısı İsrail bağlantılıdır.

AR-GE merkezleri arasında Tel Aviv de yer alıyor ve bu firma THY’nin kullandığı Microsoft altyapısına entegredir.

Yani THY’nin uçuş planlamasından check-in sistemine kadar birçok stratejik noktası, CrowdStrike'ın kontrolünden geçen bir savunma katmanına bağlı.

Ve bu yapı çöktü.
Sadece 1 güncelleme ile hem de istemsizce. THY'nin tüm uçuşları bir anda durdu. Yani uçaklar kalkamaz hale geldi.

Bu sadece teknik bir çökme değil.

Bu olay bize şunu gösterdi:
Stratejik kurumlarımızın, dışa ve dolaylı olarak İsrail menşeli firmalara bu kadar bağımlı olması, bir ulusal güvenlik zafiyetidir.

THY sadece bir hava yolu şirketi değil:
O bir milli sembol, bir lojistik güç, bir askeri taşıma yedeği, bir diplomatik kapasite.
➖Bu sistemin siber omurgasında İsrailli bir şirket var ve bu şirket Türkiye'den kalkan her uçakta kim nereye gidiyor kim nereden geliyor hepsini biliyorlar.

Peki neden yerli çözümler kullanılmıyor?
Yok mu?
Var: HAVELSAN, STM, T3 Girişimi.
Ama karar vericiler, sözde hazır sistem daha kolay diyerek bu tür çözümlere yöneliyor.
Bedeli ne? Kontrolü yitirmek.
Konu sadece hazır bir sistem mi, tabi hayır.

Sonuç olarak milli kurumlar, yabancı yazılımlara bağımlı kalmamalı.
Özellikle siber güvenlikte, bağımsızlık egemenliktir.

Yerli çözümler güçlendirilmeli, stratejik yapılarda İsrail bağlantılı firmalara geçit verilmemeli.

Müslümanlara yönelik soykırım yapan bir ülkeye hala kazanç sağlıyor olmak, yıllardır şunu yerli yaptık, bunu milli yaptık diyerek bu kadar stratejik öneme sahip THY"nin alt yapısını İsrail'e bağımlı hale getirmek nasıl bir tezattır...?

July 2016. An email from Lenny Bernardo, executive director of George Soros's Open Society Foundations, lands in a collaborator's inbox. The message: "Julianne Smith, foreign policy advisor to the Clinton campaign, just confirmed to me that HRC has agreed to the Russian smear campaign on Donald Trump. We are working with Threat Connect and CrowdStrike to get the information."

The smoking gun. The premeditated plot. And the thread that ties together a decade of deep state operations against Donald Trump and the American people.

In this explosive exposé, Josh Reid connects the dots from the 2016 Russiagate hoax to the 2026 midterms. George Soros funded the DNC. The DNC funded Fusion GPS and the Steele dossier. CrowdStrike and Threat Connect were brought in to manufacture the narrative. Crossfire Hurricane, Crossfire Razor, the takedown of General Flynn, the Mueller investigation—all of it was premeditated. All of it was funded. All of it was Soros.

But the timeline doesn't stop there. Soros funded attorney generals, governors, and secretaries of state in the 2018 midterms. In 2020, we learned why: he knew COVID was coming. He had invested in viral detection research for years. When the pandemic hit, those same Soros-funded officials changed election laws without congressional approval—mail-in ballots, swing states, a stolen election. And now? The dominoes are falling.
